result:
**Root cause:** `LuaState::intern_str` in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:1168` interned **every** string (short and long alike) into `interned_lt`. C-Lua's `luaS_newlstr` only interns strings ≤ `LUAI_MAXSHORTLEN` (40 bytes); long strings always get a fresh `TString`. So when the test built `r = gsub(s, ".", function(x) return x end)` on the 100-char `s = string.rep("a",100)`, `push_lstring("aaaa…")` returned the *same* `GcRef` as `s`, and `string.format("%p", s) ~= string.format("%p", r)` (pm.lua:436 / combined:445) failed.

**Fix:** Updated `intern_str` to mirror `luaS_newlstr` — for `bytes.len() > 40`, allocate a fresh `GcRef::new(LuaString::from_bytes(...))` without touching `interned_lt`. Short strings still intern, preserving the pointer-equality the parser/stdlib rely on.

**New state:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is clean (warnings only, no errors). One sanity run of `pm.lua` reported `PASS`. Removed no instrumentation (none was added).
